line_items.description: Tear off, haul and dispose of comp. shingles - Laminated
line_items.f9:
Our line item 1 covers insurance line item 1.

We are requesting 76.0 SQ for tear off, haul and dispose of comp. shingles - Laminated.

1. Per EagleView measured area (no waste applied to tear-off), the correct quantity is 76.0 SQ. Insurance used 75.29 SQ.

a. Please see attached EagleView Report confirming the measured square footage for Structure #1.

line_items.description: Laminated - comp. shingle rfg. - w/out felt
line_items.f9:
Our line item 2 covers insurance line item 2.

We are requesting 91.0 SQ of Laminated - comp. shingle rfg. - w/out felt.

1. The difference reflects the EagleView suggested waste calculation. Insurance used 90.67 SQ while EagleView suggests 91.0 SQ with 20% waste applied.

a. Please see attached EagleView Report for Structure #1 showing the waste calculation of 20% due to the complexity of the roof.

Our line item 3 covers insurance line item 3.

We are requesting 91.0 SQ of roofing felt - synthetic underlayment.

1. Insurance used 75.29 SQ with no waste applied to the underlayment. EagleView suggests 91.0 SQ with 20% waste for Structure #1.

a. Roofing felt, like shingles, must be installed in a staggered and overlapping pattern to ensure proper water shedding and code compliance. During this process, cuts are required at eaves, valleys, hips, ridges, and roof penetrations, which inevitably generates material waste. This waste is a necessary part of achieving a watertight underlayment system.

b. Please see attached EagleView Report confirming the 20% waste calculation for Structure #1.

Our line item 4 covers insurance line item 4.

We are requesting 471.0 LF of Asphalt starter - universal starter course.

1. The difference is $88.11. Per EagleView, the eaves starter LF for Structure #1 is 471.0 LF. Insurance used 634.37 LF which appears to combine both structures.

a. Starter is a distinct roofing component with a separate material profile and a higher associated labor rate compared to field shingles. It should not be bundled into waste calculations.

b. Please see attached EagleView Report confirming the 471.0 LF eaves measurement for Structure #1.

line_items.description: R&R Hip / Ridge cap - High profile - composition shingles
line_items.f9:
Our line item 5 covers insurance line item 5.

We are requesting 567.42 LF of R&R Hip / Ridge cap - High profile - composition shingles.

1. Per EagleView Structure #1, ridges = 138.0 LF and hips = 429.42 LF, totaling 567.42 LF. Insurance used 432.05 LF.

a. The insured currently has High Profile Ridge cap installed and it must be replaced in kind. Please see attached EagleView Report confirming the ridge and hip measurements for Structure #1.

line_items.description: R&R Exhaust cap - through roof - 6" to 8"

line_items.f9:
Our line item 13 covers insurance line item 13.

We are requesting 624.0 LF of R&R Drip edge for the main house.

1. Per EagleView Structure #1, drip edge LF is 624.0 LF. Insurance used 634.37 LF which appears to combine both structures.

a. Please see attached EagleView Report confirming the 624.0 LF drip edge measurement for Structure #1.

Our line item 15 covers insurance line item 15.

We are requesting 158.83 LF of Step flashing.

1. Per EagleView Structure #1, step flashing LF is 158.83 LF. Insurance used 158.0 LF.

a. Please see attached EagleView Report confirming the step flashing measurement for Structure #1.

b. Please see Photo Report showing the hail damage to the existing step flashing.

The insurance report left out the Valley Metal.

We are requesting 297.83 LF of R&R Valley metal.

1. Per Xactimate pricing, this costs an additional $2,289.68.

a. The insured has closed valleys which require valley metal to be replaced during the re-roof. Valley metal is a separate component from step flashing and cannot be reused once the shingles are torn off.

b. Please see attached EagleView Report confirming the 297.83 LF of valleys for Structure #1.

line_items.description: Additional charge for steep roof - greater than 12/12 slope (install)
line_items.f9:
The insurance report left out the Additional charge for steep roof install.

We are requesting 91.0 SQ for the steep roof installation charge.

1. Per Xactimate pricing, this costs an additional $4,689.23.

a. The predominant pitch per EagleView is 16/12, which exceeds the 12/12 threshold and requires a steep charge for installation. Workers must use specialized equipment, additional safety measures, and work at a significantly reduced pace on this slope.

b. Please see attached EagleView Report confirming the 16/12 pitch for Structure #1.

line_items.description: Additional charge for steep roof - greater than 12/12 slope (removal)
line_items.f9:
The insurance report left out the Additional charge for steep roof removal.

We are requesting 76.0 SQ for the steep roof removal charge.

1. Per Xactimate pricing, this costs an additional $1,221.32.

a. The predominant pitch per EagleView is 16/12, which exceeds the 12/12 threshold. Tear-off on a 16/12 pitch requires additional safety equipment and labor considerations beyond standard slope tear-off.

b. Please see attached EagleView Report confirming the 16/12 pitch for Structure #1.

line_items.description: Additional charge for high roof (2 stories or greater)
line_items.f9:
The insurance report left out the Additional charge for high roof (2 stories or greater).

We are requesting 91.0 SQ for the high roof charge.

1. Per Xactimate pricing, this costs an additional $2,227.68 for install and $593.23 for removal.

a. Per EagleView, this structure is greater than 1 story. Laborers must haul materials and equipment to an elevated height, increasing time and safety requirements.

b. Please see attached EagleView Report and Photo Report confirming the multi-story height of the structure.

The insurance report left out the Dormer specialty work.

We are requesting approval of our sub bid.

1. Our sub bid cost is $6,318.00.

a. Please see attached Photo Report showing damage.

b. Please see attached Specialty Work bid for the confirmation of price.

Our line item covers insurance line item 14.

We are requesting approval of our full sub bid to replace the chimney flashing and chimney cap.

1. The difference is $106.93. Insurance line item 14 totals $665.27. Our sub bid totals $772.20.

a. The chimney has visible cracks and the existing flashing will be fully compromised during the tear-off. The chimney cap requires an upgrade due to the deteriorated condition of the existing unit. Xactimate does not take into account the complexities of this custom chimney work.

b. Please see attached Photo Report showing the existing chimney damage and cracking.

c. Please see attached Chimney bid for confirmation of price.

The insurance report does not fully address the gutter system on this property.

We are requesting approval of our full sub bid.

1. Our sub bid cost is $12,297.75.

a. The property has a specialty gutter system including copper half-round gutters, gutter guards, and custom splash guards that require specialized installation. Xactimate does not take into account the complexities of this bespoke, high-end system.

b. The insured recently had the gutters painted and we will be repainting them to restore to pre-loss condition.

c. Splash guards are being installed in strategic locations as determined by the property owner to prevent debris buildup, but will not be placed in valley areas where debris accumulation is a concern.

d. Please see attached Photo Report showing damage to the gutter system.

e. Please see attached estimate_2249 bid for the confirmation of price.

We are requesting approval of our sub bid.

1. Our sub bid cost is $468.00.

a. Please see attached Photo Report showing damage to the windows.

b. Please see attached Estimate 8790 bid for the confirmation of price.

b. Please see attached Photo Report showing hail damage to the fence stain finish.

line_items.description: Dumpster load - Approx. 12 yards, 1-3 tons of debris
line_items.f9:
Our line item covers insurance line item 58.

We are requesting 2 dumpster loads for this project.

1. The difference is $564.16. Insurance line item 58 totals $385.50 for 1 load. We are requesting $949.66 for 2 loads.

a. Given the total roof area of approximately 85 squares across both the main house and detached garage, plus the steep 16/12 pitch and complexity of the roof system, a single dumpster load is insufficient to contain all tear-off debris.

b. Please see attached Photo Report showing the volume of roofing material on the property.

line_items.description: Residential Supervision / Project Management - per hour
line_items.f9:
The insurance report left out Residential Supervision / Project Management.

We are requesting 8.0 HR of project management.

1. Per Xactimate pricing, this costs an additional $564.08.

a. This project involves coordination of multiple trades including roofing, gutters, specialty metalwork, pergola, fencing, painting, and chimney work. A dedicated project manager is required to schedule, sequence, and supervise each trade to ensure code-compliant and quality installation.

b. Insurance restoration projects require additional documentation, inspection scheduling, supplemental negotiations, and warranty coordination beyond standard construction management.

The insurance report left out the Gutter labor minimum.

We are requesting 1 EA for the gutter labor minimum.

1. Per Xactimate pricing, this costs an additional $336.20.

a. A labor minimum applies when the total gutter scope requires mobilization, setup, and teardown that exceeds the per-linear-foot pricing captured in the gutter line items.

For decades we have vetted, managed, trained and warrantied subcontractors for roofing, gutters, painting, fencing, siding etc. due to storm restoration in the DFW area.

We charge a minimum 20% overhead and profit above the subcontractors cost as we act as the single point of contact for all subcontractors' trade complexities, plus cover all their liability and warranty for our clients.

Project Requires General Contracting
This project involves coordination of multiple trades—including roofing, gutter replacement, exterior painting, window screen repair, specialty metalwork, chimney, fencing, pergola, and pillar restoration. Per industry standards and Xactimate guidelines, O&P is warranted whenever three or more trades are involved and coordinated by a general contractor.

Complex Scheduling and Management
Each trade must be scheduled in the correct sequence, managed for quality and timing, and supervised for code compliance and safety. This requires administrative staff, project managers, and dedicated personnel which all reside at our physical office—all part of our company's operational infrastructure.

We Are Not a Single-Trade Contractor
Unlike subcontractors, we do not perform only one scope of work. We maintain a physical office, estimators, coordinators, insurance claim specialists, and field supervisors. These are all actual employees of IFC Contracting Solutions—these employees are the overhead which is built into O&P. These costs are necessary to support project execution, client communication, and warranty management.

Insurance Restoration Demands Administrative Oversight
Insurance jobs require extra documentation, invoicing, inspection scheduling, supplemental negotiation, and warranty coordination, all of which involve personnel beyond the jobsite. These employees reside at our physical location and work to adhere to the highest construction standards. This is not reimbursed in trade line items and is accounted for within O&P.
